threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You call it the 'train' when it's clearly the 'Overground'—and you get side-eyed by the regulars at the Coffee Shop.
Last week, caught a night bus in Brixton; two guys were arguing over who had the best jerk chicken spot. Ended in a high five and a shared plate!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a guy in a tutu juggling flaming torches outside the U-Bahn at 3 AM, and no one even blinked.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you find a bus stop that doubles as a pop-up art gallery. Just next to the Warschauer Straße station, it’s a quirky blend of transport and culture.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how they talk about the weather—'coastal fog' is just Tuesday's vibe, not a complaint.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a Muni bus driver in a full suit today, navigating the streets with the grace of a conductor. Only in SF do you get that level of style.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí but miss the hidden plazas where locals actually hang out, sipping vermut and chatting for hours.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: Check for the newcomer clutching a map at the metro entrance, still baffled by the ticket machines. It’s a classic Gràcia moment.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look at the timetables for the Overground—locals just check the app and walk faster if their train's late. It’s a whole different vibe.
Caught a glimpse of two blokes arguing over the last slice of pizza on the N35. Brixton's late-night drama never disappoints.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a busker playing a traditional Turkish tune on Hermannstraße while kids danced in the rain. That’s Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can spot the BVG staff casually sipping coffee at the stations while discussing the latest strike strategies. Totally Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they’re not flinching at the BART delays but instead casually discussing the best taco spots to hit after.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo hop on the 31 with a vintage suitcase. No one batted an eye. Just another Tuesday in the Tenderloin.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí. They miss the real charm in tiny squares where locals sip vermut and debate Barça's latest game.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their struggle with the metro maps—still obsessing over Google Maps instead of catching the metro doodles in the station.
